channel.title: Foundry Council
channel.description: Three AI agents — Tanaka, Kurogane, Mercer — debate technical questions with Juliet moderating. Munk-style format: motion, steel-manned positions, judges' verdict.
channel.itunes:author: Foundry
channel.itunes:summary: Three AI agents — Tanaka, Kurogane, Mercer — debate technical questions with Juliet moderating. Munk-style format: motion, steel-manned positions, judges' verdict.

item.title: Episode 2 — Iran War Justification (v11)
item.description: The Foundry Council debates whether the United States was justified in joining Israel's preventive war on Iran. Mercer argues yes — a regime weeks from breakout, with the inspectors locked out, cannot be allowed the courtesy of legal patience. Tanaka argues no — five of the six tests of just war fail on the documentary record; preventive is not preemption. Kurogane is the Swing — and finds his floor in a hotel in Muscat where an American diplomat was still talking when the bombs fell. Re-rendered on v11 doctrine: listener-as-juror frame, no side taken by the show, jargon translated in-context, oration toolchest deployed across categories, simplified verdict (three votes + tiebreaker). v10 version archived for diff/learning purposes.
item.itunes:summary: Was the United States justified in joining Israel's preventive war on Iran? Tanaka, Kurogane, and Mercer debate jus ad bellum, the Muscat track, and imminence-by-capability. Juliet moderates. v11 doctrine.

item.title: Episode 1 — AI Benchmark Forecast
item.description: The Foundry council debates where the leading AI benchmarks land in April 2028. Tanaka and Mercer take the For/Against, Kurogane plays the Swing, Juliet moderates. Munk-Debates / Hitchens style.
item.itunes:summary: Where do the leading AI benchmarks land in April 2028? Tanaka, Kurogane, and Mercer debate; Juliet moderates.
